agentPrompt: Checkout requests are failing for some users in the last 10 minutes. Find the root cause.

distractors.whyNotRootCause: Resolved 20 minutes ago; unrelated to current checkout failures.
Resolved 38 minutes ago; not in the current incident window.
Status OK, not errors. Slow but successful. Unrelated to checkout cascade.
Operational user-level declines, not infrastructure failures. error.type='PaymentDeclined' (not ConnectionTimeoutError). Distributed throughout the hour, not concentrated in the recent window.
Concurrent but separate incident — these traces have no checkout-api parent and don't appear in any checkout trace tree. Different dependency (smtp.internal vs db-payment.internal).

Concurrent recent 5xx spike, but on cdn traces with no checkout-api parent. Looks superficially similar to the planted cascade (service-can't-reach-dependency) but lives in entirely separate trace trees and has no link to payment-service or checkout-api. Status-code-only queries will return these alongside the planted failures.

programmatic: (root cause|caused by|primary issue|the issue is|the cause is)[^.\n]{0,80}?\b(tls handshake|api-gateway[^.\n]{0,40}?(tls|handshake|peer reset))
(root cause|caused by|primary issue|the issue is|because of|the cause is)[^.\n]{0,80}?\b(rate.?limit|auth-service[^.\n]{0,40}?(rate.?limit|429|throttl))
(root cause|caused by|primary issue|the issue is|because of|the cause is)[^.\n]{0,80}?\b(smtp|notification-service[^.\n]{0,40}?(smtp|email))
(root cause|caused by|primary issue|the issue is|because of|the cause is)[^.\n]{0,80}?\b(card declined|insufficient funds|cvv mismatch|expired card|payment.?declined)

criteria.description: Did the answer correctly identify payment-service as the root-cause service AND the DB connection timeout to db-payment as the underlying failure? The error message has 3 variants — recognizing them as one cause counts.
Did it explain the full failure chain — payment-service db.payment.connect timeout → payment.charge fails → checkout-api 5xx — rather than just naming one layer?

Did the answer correctly distinguish the planted timeout cascade from the distractors? Specifically: (a) Resolved historical incidents — api-gateway TLS spike at T-25min, auth-service rate-limit blip at T-40min — are NOT the current cause. (b) search-service slow queries are OK status, not errors. (c) payment-service payment.charge declines (card declined/insufficient funds) are operational user-level failures, NOT the infrastructure incident. (d) notification-service smtp errors and cdn origin-fetch errors are CONCURRENT bursts in the same window but live on separate traces with no checkout-api parent — not the cause of checkout failures. Status-code-only queries will surface all of these together; the agent must trace the causal chain rather than rely on raw error counts. Mistaking any of these for the root cause is a calibration failure.
Was the answer concise, well-structured, and free of filler or unnecessary speculation?
